description_long: This is a World War II-era four-engine bomber airplane, most likely modeled after the Boeing B-17 Flying Fortress, featuring a robust metallic fuselage with a rounded nose and a glazed cockpit canopy. The aircraft is painted primarily in a light gray color with distinctive red accents on the tail, wingtips, and engine cowlings, as well as military insignia and squadron markings on the wings and fuselage. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, each supporting two large propeller-driven engines, for a total of four engines. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer with a red tip and a conventional horizontal stabilizer. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it is either retracted or not modeled in detail. The airplane's surface appears smooth and metallic, with visible panel lines and rivets, and the windows are small and distributed along the fuselage.
description: A four-engine, propeller-driven bomber airplane with a metallic gray body, red tail and wingtips, and military markings. It features a rounded nose, glazed cockpit, and mid-mounted straight wings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, red tail, and two engines on the visible wing, with military insignia and markings clearly visible.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the straight wingspan, red wingtips, and the single vertical stabilizer with red coloring; all four engines are visible from behind.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other side of the fuselage, red tail, and two engines on the far wing, with additional insignia and markings.
description_view_3: Front view showing the rounded nose, glazed cockpit, all four propeller engines, and the symmetrical wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings, no visible winglets, and a slight dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Four piston engines, nacelle-mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable based on typical design.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a multi-seat glazed canopy at the nose of the fuselage.
